title: EEG: Depression rest
description: This dataset comprises resting-state EEG recordings from 122 college-age participants with varying levels of depressive symptoms as measured by the Beck Depression Inventory. Participants completed two 6-minute resting sessions with eyes-open and eyes-closed conditions, with the first session occurring immediately after EEG setup and the second following approximately one hour of task performance. The data were collected between 2008-2010 and include clinical interview information for a subset of participants.
methods_description: EEG data were collected during two 6-minute resting sessions per participant. The first session occurred immediately after EEG electrode placement, and the second session followed approximately one hour of task performance. Each session included alternating eyes-open and eyes-closed conditions in 1-minute blocks, with triggers marking the instructed condition spans. Participants were selected based on Beck Depression Inventory scores to represent reliably high or low depression phenotypes.
